channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-28
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In **scienceverse/metacheck**, the `dev` branch has been updated with a new feature to flag p-values reported as exactly zero and a rewritten OSF prereg extraction using a "schema-driven dispatch" approach. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** has released version 2.4.98 with fixes to tables and updates to "η²p-from-structure" and "r-cell + bracket-CI". **Davidvandijcke/coarse** has made notable strides, localizing the confirmation email and review page, and enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table. Additionally, **JamieCummins/regcheck** has seen updates to its report render, multistudy cli, and local reporting, with improvements based on community suggestions. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made towards advancing open-source research tools.

28 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

metacheck10 commits
regcheck10 commits
docpluck7 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: regcheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The report render was updated, as stated in the commit message "update report render". Additionally, changes were made based on suggestions from Matilda, with the commit message noting "improvements based on Matilda suggestions", although the specifics of these changes are not detailed. The multistudy cli also received attention, with "finishing touches" being added, according to the commit message. Earlier in the sequence, local reporting was improved, with the commit message stating "improve local reporting".

3 commit(s) today:

  • d5e2697 [staging] update report render — Jamie Cummins
  • e3f869b [staging] improvements based on Matilda suggestions — Jamie Cummins
  • c682ed0 [staging] add finishing touches on multistudy cli — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: activity update
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized it with the `escicheckapp` repository at commit `f37b5a1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.6`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)". This commit was associated with issue `#20`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The commit brought the `main` branch in line with the specified version of `effectcheck`. The synchronization commit updated the `main` branch to match the `escicheckapp` repository at the specified commit hash.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was included, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The revision of the validation note and addition of a citation are the specific changes made in this commit. The commit message does not mention any other changes or additions to the repository. There are no other commits to report on, as only one commit is provided. The work was limited to the `main` branch, with no other branches being updated.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
